meaning: n.[哲]虚无主义,极端怀疑论
examples: Well, I would say one ends up in nihilism.
Its nihilism and its unbalanced lack of basic trust are haunting.
True, there is some ugly nihilism mixed in with voters' legitimate grievances.
The latter may be an outgrowth of the very nihilism that guides destructive fantasy into action.
As supercool young directors go, Tarantino (whose first film this is) is fairly engaging: his nihilism is antic and oddly cheery.
